definition: An angle that measures less than ninety degrees.
example: An angle of 45 degrees is an acute angle.

definition: The enclosing boundary of a curved geometric figure, especially a circle.
example: You can calculate the circumference using the radius.

definition: A solid or hollow object which tapers from a circular or roughly circular base to a point.
example: Ice cream is often served in a cone.

definition: Identical in form; coinciding exactly when superimposed.
example: Two triangles are congruent if all their corresponding sides and angles are equal.

definition: A symmetrical three-dimensional shape, either solid or hollow, contained by six equal squares.
example: A standard die is shaped like a cube.

definition: A solid geometric figure with straight parallel sides and a circular or oval cross section.
example: A soda can is shaped like a cylinder.

definition: A unit of measurement of angles.
example: A full circle is 360 degrees.

definition: The line segment where two faces of a solid figure meet.
example: A cube has twelve distinct edges.

definition: Any of the individual flat surfaces of a solid object.
example: A cube has six square faces.

definition: A polygon with six straight sides and angles.
example: Honeycombs are built in the shape of a hexagon.

definition: An angle that measures more than 90 degrees but less than 180 degrees.
example: An angle of 120 degrees is an obtuse angle.

definition: A polygon with eight straight sides and eight angles.
example: A stop sign is shaped like an octagon.

definition: A four-sided plane rectilinear figure with opposite sides parallel.
example: Every rectangle is a parallelogram.

definition: A polygon with five straight sides and five angles.
example: The US Department of Defense headquarters is shaped like a pentagon.

definition: A solid geometric figure whose two end faces are similar, equal, and parallel rectilinear figures, and whose sides are parallelograms.
example: A triangular prism splits light into a rainbow.

definition: A monumental structure with a square or triangular base and sloping sides that meet in a point at the top.
example: The ancient Egyptians built many a large pyramid.

definition: A four-sided polygon.
example: Rectangles and squares are types of quadrilaterals.

definition: A quadrilateral all of whose sides have the same length.
example: A rhombus looks like a tilted square.

definition: An angle of exactly 90 degrees.
example: The corners of a square form a right angle.

definition: A round solid figure, or its surface, with every point on its surface equidistant from its center.
example: The Earth is shaped roughly like a sphere.

definition: A straight line or plane that touches a curve or curved surface at a point, but if extended does not cross it at that point.
example: The tangent line touches the circle at exactly one point.

definition: A quadrilateral with at least one pair of parallel sides.
example: A trapezoid has one set of parallel opposite sides.

definition: The highest point; the top or apex. In geometry, the point where two lines meet to form an angle.
example: A triangle has three vertices, one for each corner.
